marketState: bearish-leaning, low-conviction — price already depressed vs seasonal median but confirmed by bearish technicals and storage surplus
summary: Price sits 11.9pp below its seasonal median and 15.3% below typical YTD performance, while storage is +184 Bcf above the 5-year average despite it being cooling (summer AC demand) season — a clear oversupply signal. Managed money is net short (-60,295 contracts, 35th percentile of 5y), and every technical timeframe from 5-minute to weekly shows a bearish trend with a 50<200 EMA death cross, though the daily RSI (33.6) and close below the lower Bollinger band hint at short-term oversold conditions. Fair value at $2.917 (band $2.417-$3.417) puts the front contract essentially at fair (-$0.03 spread), offering no immediate mispricing edge, and forward fair-value readings (7/14/21-day), the futures curve, and categorized near-term weather demand data (1-7D/8-16D) are all missing from this snapshot, which caps conviction. Seasonality itself carries a 'Low' reliability flag (50pp spread), so the median-deviation signal should be weighted but not over-trusted. Net read: bearish tilt on the multi-week horizon from storage/seasonality/technicals, but no clean short-term catalyst given fair-value neutrality and missing forward/weather inputs.

longTerm.rationale: Price is running well below its seasonal median (-11.9pp / -15.3% YTD vs -3.4% typical) with weak odds of being higher in 4 weeks (31%) and a typical -3.1% 4-week seasonal move; storage is +184 Bcf above the 5y average despite summer demand season, and managed money is already net short, all pointing the same bearish direction toward the fair-value band's lower half.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained daily close back above $3.15 (above fair-value midpoint and the bearish EMA cross zone), or a storage deficit surprise / sharp upside revision to the weather demand outlook that flips seasonality's probabilities materially higher.

shortTerm.rationale: Front price is essentially at fair value (-$0.03 spread, status FAIR) so there is no near-term mispricing edge, and while every technical timeframe reads bearish trend, the daily RSI (33.6) and below-lower-Bollinger close signal oversold risk of a short-covering bounce; forward fair-value (7/14/21D), the futures curve, and categorized 1-7D/8-16D weather demand data are all missing, removing the inputs that would normally sharpen a short-horizon call.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) a daily close below $2.80 confirming the bearish breakout while RSI still has room lower, giving a clean short trigger, or (b) a bounce/rejection into the $2.95-$3.05 zone (near fair value / prior EMA resistance) to sell with defined risk. Also wait for forward fair-value, futures-curve, and 1-7D/8-16D weather demand fields to populate before sizing up conviction.
